item.title: About
item.description: Hi there My name is Ian (@m1ithryn). Security analyst, system administrator, and at times, even a developer. I run this blog as an outlet for research, a place to store notes, and a way to learn. Interested in offensive security. Contact Me: Twitter 🐦 Github 💻 Email 📫 GPG $gpg --fingerprint --list-keys gpg@ianmckay.33mail.com pub rsa4096 2020-06-21 [SC] E08D 4AAE B48D 92CA EF88 C271 4124 4D04 2615 4CFC uid [ultimate] Ian Mckay <gpg@ianmckay.

item.title: Hello World
item.description: Hello everyone. Im excited to share that I have finally gotten around to creating a security focused blog site (Name TBD). My intent for this is to be an outlet for my research, a place to store my notes, and potentially a place that helps people learn more about various security disciplines. Stay Tuned!!!

Recommendations These are some of my favorite books, podcasts, blogs, news sites, and more! This is not a complete list, and will be continually updated. Books Attacking Network Protocols - James Forshaw Real-World Bug Hunting - Peter Yaworski Hacking Exposed 7 - Stuart McClure / Joel Scambray / George Kurtz Practical Malware Analysis - Michael Sikorski / Andrew Honig Hacking: The Art of Exploitation - Jon Erickson The Hacker Playbook 3 - Peter Kim Hash Crack: Password Cracking Manual (v3) - Joshua Picolet Red Team Field Manual - Ben Clark Crypto 101 - Laurens Van Houtven BulletProof SSL and TLS - Ivan Ristić Windows Internals I - Pavel Yosifovich Windows Internals II - Pavel Yosifovich Black Hat Python - Justin Seitz Black Hat Go - Tom Steele / Chris Patten / Dan Kottmann Podcasts Risky Business - Patrick Gray / Adam Boileau Darknet Diaries - Jack Rhysider Training Hack The Box OverTheWire Vulnhub PortSwigger WebSecurity Academy DetectionLab JuiceShop Research Blogs MalwareUnicorn Azeria Labs ADSecurity Daniel Miessler Schneier On Security News Slashdot BleepingComputer US-CERT Hacker News Conference Talks Weaponizing Your Pets: The War Kitteh and the Denial of Service Dog - Gene Bransfield HAKC THE POLICE - Bill Swearingen Jackpotting Automated Teller Machines Redux - Barnaby Jack Pwned By The Owner: What Happens When You Steal A Hacker’s Computer - Zoz Steal Everything, Kill Everyone, Cause Total Financial Ruin!

RTL-SDR This weekend I finally recieved my RTL-SDR USB dongle in the mail. It was recommended to me by a coworker as a great way to break into SDR, without having to pay for something like the HackRF or BladeRF. What is RTL-SDR? rtl-sdr.com explains the following: RTL-SDR is a very cheap ~$25 USB dongle that can be used as a computer based radio scanner for receiving live radio signals in your area (no internet required).
